title: Legal Copilot Indian Contract Review Book

rules.label: Payment terms

references.note: Performance should follow the agreed timeline, and unclear payment timing makes enforcement harder.

rules.reviewFocus: Check whether payment dates, invoice triggers, and late payment consequences are clearly written.
rules.warning: Vague payment timing or a one-sided right to withhold payment can create collection risk for a small business.

rules.label: Termination rights

references.note: A party affected by non-performance or breach should have clear notice and remedy language.

rules.reviewFocus: Check whether the notice period, cure period, and compensation for work already performed are clearly defined.
rules.warning: Immediate or discretionary termination without notice can leave the weaker party unpaid after investing time or money.

rules.label: Liability cap

references.note: Risk allocation should be clear when damages or compensation are triggered by breach.

rules.reviewFocus: Check whether liability is capped and whether indirect or consequential losses are excluded fairly.
rules.warning: Unlimited indemnity or an uncapped damages clause can create exposure far beyond the contract value.

rules.label: Dispute resolution

references.note: The arbitration clause must be clear about the process, seat, and how disputes move to arbitration.

rules.reviewFocus: Check whether the arbitration seat, language, costs, and court jurisdiction are clearly stated.
rules.warning: A vague dispute clause can force the smaller party into an expensive or one-sided process.

rules.label: Confidentiality

references.note: Confidentiality promises should have scope, duration, and breach consequences that can actually be enforced.

rules.reviewFocus: Check whether confidential information is defined and whether exceptions are fair and mutual.
rules.warning: A broad confidentiality clause can block normal business use if it is not limited by purpose, time, and scope.

rules.label: Intellectual property

references.note: Ownership and assignment of creative work should be explicit instead of implied.
Assignment or transfer terms should be clearly agreed and supported by consideration.

rules.reviewFocus: Check whether the contract clearly says who owns work created during the deal and what is licensed back.
rules.warning: If IP ownership is vague, the other party may claim rights over work you create or pay for.

rules.label: Governing law

references.note: The governing law clause should not conflict with mandatory Indian law requirements.

rules.reviewFocus: Check whether the contract names the correct governing law and forum for disputes.
rules.warning: A foreign forum or unclear jurisdiction clause can increase cost and delay if a dispute happens.

rules.label: Force majeure

references.note: Impossible performance and frustration should be addressed clearly, not left to vague discretion.

rules.reviewFocus: Check whether the clause lists qualifying events, notice duties, and suspension or termination rights.
rules.warning: If force majeure is vague, the stronger party may use it to avoid obligations without a clear trigger.

rules.label: Amendment and entire agreement

references.note: Changes to contract terms should be documented clearly so later disputes do not rely on oral promises.

rules.reviewFocus: Check whether changes must be written and whether side promises are excluded or preserved properly.
rules.warning: If amendment rules are loose, one side can later deny or rewrite the deal using informal instructions.
